primary.title: C-GenReg: Training-Free 3D Point Cloud Registration by Multi-View-Consistent Geometry-to-Image Generation with Probabilistic Modalities Fusion
primary.abstract: We introduce C-GenReg, a training-free framework for 3D point cloud registration that leverages the complementary strengths of world-scale generative priors and registration-oriented Vision Foundation Models (VFMs). Current learning-based 3D point cloud registration methods struggle to generalize across sensing modalities, sampling differences, and environments. Hence, C-GenReg augments the geometric point cloud registration branch by transferring the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain, where VFMs excel, using a World Foundation Model to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations from the input geometry. This generative transfer, preserves spatial coherence across source and target views without any fine-tuning. From these generated views, a VFM pretrained for finding dense correspondences extracts matches. The resulting pixel correspondences are lifted back to 3D via the original depth maps. To further enhance robustness, we introduce a"Match-then-Fuse"probabilistic cold-fusion scheme that combines two independent correspondence posteriors, that of the generated-RGB branch with that of the raw geometric branch. This principled fusion preserves each modality inductive bias and provides calibrated confidence without any additional learning. C-GenReg is zero-shot and plug-and-play: all modules are pretrained and operate without fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on indoor (3DMatch, ScanNet) and outdoor (Waymo) benchmarks demonstrate strong zero-shot performance and superior cross-domain generalization. For the first time, we demonstrate a generative registration framework that operates successfully on real outdoor LiDAR data, where no imagery data is available.

Current learning-based 3D point cloud registration methods struggle to generalize across sensing modalities, sampling differences, and environments.

C-GenReg transfers the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain by using a World Foundation Model to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations from input geometry. A VFM pretrained for dense correspondences extracts matches from these views, and pixel correspondences are lifted back to 3D via depth maps. A 'Match-then-Fuse' probabilistic cold-fusion scheme combines correspondence posteriors from the generated-RGB and raw geometric branches.

competitors.title: Assessing the practical applicability of neural‐based point clouds registration algorithms: A comparative analysis
competitors.abstract: Abstract Point cloud registration is a vital task in three‐dimensional (3D) perception, with several different applications robotics. Recent advancements have introduced neural‐based techniques that promise enhanced accuracy and robustness. In this paper, we thoroughly evaluate well‐known point methods using the Clouds Registration Benchmark, which was developed to cover large variety of use cases. Our evaluation focuses on performance these when applied real‐complex data, presents more challenging realistic scenario than simpler experiments typically conducted by original authors. The results reveal considerable variability across techniques, highlighting importance assessing algorithms settings. Notably, 3DSmoothNet emerges as standout solution, demonstrating good consistent various data sets. Its efficacy, coupled relatively low graphics processing unit (GPU) memory footprint, makes it promising choice for robotics applications, even if not yet suitable real‐time due its execution time. Fully Convolutional Geometric Features also performs well, albeit greater among PREDATOR GeoTransformer are promising, but demand substantial GPU memory, handling clouds from Benchmark. A notable finding concerns Fast Feature Histograms, exhibit comparable best approaches while demanding minimal computational resources. Overall, comparative analysis provides valuable insights into strengths limitations both terms quality resources required. This helps researchers make informed decisions applications.

hf_2604.16680.title: C-GenReg: Training-Free 3D Point Cloud Registration by Multi-View-Consistent Geometry-to-Image Generation with Probabilistic Modalities Fusion
hf_2604.16680.abstract: We introduce C-GenReg, a training-free framework for 3D point cloud registration that leverages the complementary strengths of world-scale generative priors and registration-oriented Vision Foundation Models (VFMs). Current learning-based 3D point cloud registration methods struggle to generalize across sensing modalities, sampling differences, and environments. Hence, C-GenReg augments the geometric point cloud registration branch by transferring the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain, where VFMs excel, using a World Foundation Model to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations from the input geometry. This generative transfer, preserves spatial coherence across source and target views without any fine-tuning. From these generated views, a VFM pretrained for finding dense correspondences extracts matches. The resulting pixel correspondences are lifted back to 3D via the original depth maps. To further enhance robustness, we introduce a"Match-then-Fuse"probabilistic cold-fusion scheme that combines two independent correspondence posteriors, that of the generated-RGB branch with that of the raw geometric branch. This principled fusion preserves each modality inductive bias and provides calibrated confidence without any additional learning. C-GenReg is zero-shot and plug-and-play: all modules are pretrained and operate without fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on indoor (3DMatch, ScanNet) and outdoor (Waymo) benchmarks demonstrate strong zero-shot performance and superior cross-domain generalization. For the first time, we demonstrate a generative registration framework that operates successfully on real outdoor LiDAR data, where no imagery data is available.

C-GenReg — A training-free 3D point cloud registration method that transfers the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain.
World Foundation Model — A model used to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations from input geometry.
Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) — Models that achieve remarkable robustness by training on massive, heterogeneous datasets.
Match-then-Fuse — A probabilistic cold-fusion scheme that combines correspondence posteriors from the generated-RGB and raw geometric branches.
RoITr — A method that embedded Point-Pair-Feature coordinates into a transformer backbone to achieve rotation invariance and distinctive geometric correspondences.
3DMatch — An indoor RGB-D benchmark for 3D point cloud registration.
ScanNet — An indoor RGB-D benchmark for 3D point cloud registration.
Waymo — An outdoor LiDAR benchmark for 3D point cloud registration.
Zero-shot — A setting where a model is evaluated on a new task or domain without any additional training.
Cross-domain generalization — The ability of a model to perform well across different domains, such as indoor and outdoor scenes.
Probabilistic modalities fusion — A method that combines multiple modalities, such as geometry and image, using probabilistic techniques.
C-GenReg is a training-free framework for 3D point cloud registration
C-GenReg leverages world-scale generative priors and registration-oriented Vision Foundation Models (VFMs)
C-GenReg transfers the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain

committee_synthesis:
C-GenReg, a novel approach to 3D point cloud registration, marks a significant departure from traditional methods by leveraging a World Foundation Model to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations. This enables the transfer of the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain, effectively sidestepping the need for extensive training data. By harnessing Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) pretrained for dense correspondences, C-GenReg efficiently extracts matches from views, which are then lifted back to 3D via depth maps.

The core of C-GenReg's innovation lies in its 'Match-then-Fuse' probabilistic cold-fusion scheme, which adeptly combines correspondence posteriors from both generated-RGB and raw geometric branches. This method not only enhances the accuracy of 3D point cloud registration but also demonstrates a remarkable ability to operate effectively in zero-shot scenarios across various benchmarks, including indoor RGB-D benchmarks like 3DMatch and ScanNet, as well as outdoor LiDAR data from Waymo.

A critical analysis of C-GenReg reveals a tension between the specificity of scene descriptions and the performance of the registration process. Interestingly, the method shows resilience even when detailed scene descriptions are replaced with general ones, with negligible degradation in performance. Moreover, it maintains reasonably strong performance even with minimal prompts. However, the introduction of semantically incorrect prompts significantly degrades registration accuracy, highlighting the importance of accurate prior knowledge in the generation process.

For readers, the key takeaway is that C-GenReg presents a promising, training-free solution for 3D point cloud registration, capitalizing on the strengths of World Foundation Models and VFMs. Its ability to achieve state-of-the-art zero-shot results across diverse benchmarks, coupled with its robustness to variations in scene descriptions, positions C-GenReg as a versatile and effective tool for applications requiring accurate 3D registration.
